danger_signals.meaning: Game running on unauthorized cartridge copier hardware

danger_signals.visual_form: Region error message on boot (NTSC vs PAL incompatibility)
danger_signals.meaning: Game cartridge region incompatible with console hardware

safety_signals.visual_form: Game proceeds past boot check to main menu
safety_signals.meaning: Running on official Nintendo cartridge with no SRAM present

safety_signals.visual_form: Game boots normally without error messages
safety_signals.meaning: Game cartridge region compatible with console hardware

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bullet-Proof Software branding (replaced in later versions)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Copyright screen displays licensing authority; BPS logo removed in international rerelease
Appears in Japanese version and original release copyright screens; absent in later Nintendo-managed versions
Updated copyright screen in rerelease versions reflects transfer of Tetris licensing authority

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Game running on legitimate official cartridge
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No error message; game proceeds to title screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Anti-piracy SRAM write attempt fails silently, allowing normal game flow

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Anti-piracy error message displayed on boot
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: SRAM write succeeds (cartridge copier has SRAM), triggering error display and game halt

world_state_to_visual.world_state: NTSC cartridge on PAL console (or vice versa)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Region error message displayed on boot
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game halts and does not proceed to menu

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen displays with appropriate regional variant (JP: blue background with animation; International: black background static)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game proceeds normally to main menu

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: BPS logo visible on copyright screen; Bullet-Proof Software credited as Tetris licensor
Copyright screen updated to reflect new licensing authority; BPS logo removed

environmental_storytelling.example: Rerelease removed BPS logo and updated legal text to reflect The Tetris Company's establishment in 1996, visually communicating shift in licensing authority and corporate governance
Japanese version displays animated blue-background title sequence; international versions use static black background, signaling localization level and regional branding priority
Anti-piracy and region-check error messages communicate hardware authentication state and technical compatibility without gameplay context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: SRAM write attempt fails; game proceeds normally
SRAM write succeeds; anti-piracy error triggered; game halts
Game boots and displays region-appropriate title screen
Game halts at boot with region error message

notes: Tetris 2 (SNES 1993) is a puzzle game with gameplay mechanics similar to Dr. Mario but using tetrominoes. The game's visual communication of world-state is minimal and focused on hardware authentication and regional compatibility rather than narrative or environmental storytelling. No faction dynamics, danger/safety signals, or political themes exist within the game world itself. All manifestation patterns relate to anti-piracy/DRM systems and regional localization. Debug menu content (accessible via Pro Action Replay) reveals unused cutscenes (Baking/Boxing) and references to animal-based opponent selection (Turtle, Dog, Rabbit) suggesting earlier development direction. Regional differences are primarily cosmetic (title screen animation, logo presence). This analysis reflects the puzzle game paradigm with minimal world-state complexity.
sources_used: tcrf - Tetris 2 (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
